_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
2/28/26 11:45 AM Update:  Runs are starting to soften nicely! Spring vibes are in full force today with mostly sunny skies and warm temps! Grab your sunscreen, dark lenses, and your favorite Après outfit! Off-piste remains variable and is transitioning to spring snow. Continue to be aware of a crust that has formed on some solar, un-groomed aspects. Last week's new snow has allowed our teams to continue to open terrain and rebuild our groomed runs. Although our team is hard at work, please obey all rope lines, closures, and signage for your own safety and that of our mountain operations personnel. We look forward to opening more terrain as conditions and avalanche safety allow. Stop by Crystal Mountain Outfitters for our Spring 30% off sale! Night skiing runs 'till 7pm on Gold Hills, Quicksilver, and Discovery. Happy Saturday and we'll see you on the mountain!

Rafters and Fireside Cantina are closed for the day.

Terrain Parks: The Discovery Park, Arwine's Jump Line and the new Sudden Drop Park (Lower Arwine's) are open today!

Events:
  • Today! Head on up top for our Retro Dreams summit party for a live DJ and giveaways from noon to 3pm, and stick around for Summit Apres: Shuckfest from 3-5pm. This free, all-ages shuck-and-celebrate showdown takes place at the Summit House - aka the best perch on Crystal Mountain to kick back after carving up the slopes. 
  • Get eyes on the Throne from 10am to 2pm for our Freeride World Qualifier and watch the best freeride athletes in North America compete for a spot on the Freeride World Tour!

Notes for today:
  1. Summit House will be closed March 11 and Rafters will be closed March 11 and 12 for private events. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. Night Skiing hours have been adjusted to 4:00 p.m. to 7 p.m.
  4. We’ve decided to discontinue the parking waitlist for the 25/26 season. Parking reservations are back to first-come, first-served basis, and still required on weekends and holidays
  5. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  6. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  7. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  8. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  9. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 2/28/2026 3:30pm 

Forecaster: Bradford Swanson

Synopsis: Happy March everyone! Spring might not technically be until March 20th, but it sure felt like spring today. This pattern will continue through Sunday before cooling off early Tuesday morning. Expect some high clouds over the next couple of days with light winds and very warm temps. Dig out your spring fit and enjoy the nice weather. 

Saturday Night:
Sky Cover: Mostly clear
Freezing Elevation: 8,500’
12hr Precip: None
Wind Trends: Light, West
Sunday:
Sky Cover: Mostly clear
Freezing Elevation: 8,500’
12hr Precip: None
Wind Trends: Light, West
Sunday Night:
Sky Cover: Clear
Freezing Elevation: 8,500’
12hr Precip: None
Wind Trends: Light, North

Extended: Tuesday, a cold front passes through the region, bringing a decent dose of snow. Stay tuned as this storm gets closer; it could be a pretty good one. 


Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification

_inspectorSnowReport.Alert:
Gold Hills is open for night skiing today, 2/28. Southback remains closed. The Northway will remain closed due to snow conditions. 

Uphill Travel Status: PERMITTED via standard route through the meadow.
